narrative: Dear [Student's Name], imagine the admissions team looking at your application like they're piecing together the story of your journey to becoming a lawyer, drawing from patterns they've seen in thousands of students who’ve walked this path before. Your story starts strong in their eyes with your LSAT score of 36, but it quickly faces headwinds from your undergraduate GPA of 2.8, which whispered doubts about your academic stamina from the outset. What really weighed heaviest, though, were your class rankings—landing in the 2nd decile both in Year 1 and Year 3—which stood out as the strongest signals pulling toward concerns about passing the bar, suggesting those early and later struggles might carry forward. Your middle family income added another layer, as it often means fewer resources for the intense prep that top performers lean on. Even your background as a white male in a full-time program, while not uncommon, aligned with profiles where the model has seen higher hurdles to clearing the bar exam. Layered together, these pieces painted a picture of a 78.9% chance of not making it through, based on how similar stories have unfolded. It's not that you're without potential—your LSAT shows real aptitude—but the model saw the rankings and GPA as the biggest risks, dimming the overall outlook. This isn't a judgment on your character or drive; it's just the tough math of predicting success in such a high-stakes field. If you can boost those academic deciles or build more momentum in future steps, your story could shift dramatically. You've got the spark—keep refining it. We're rooting for your next chapter.
